description: Beta/prod deployment infrastructure, CI/CD split into per-service workflows, UX fix for Burn Squad creation, and post-deployment integration + E2E tests.

userStories.title: Remove 'add friends first' blocker from Burn Squad creation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a Burn Squad without having friends yet, so that I can set up my squad name and schedule first and invite people later.
acceptanceCriteria: When the user has no friends, the Burn Squad creation page shows an informational message such as 'No friends to invite yet — you can add them after creating the squad' but does NOT show a blocking 'Add friends first' link that implies they cannot proceed
The 'Create Burn Squad' button is enabled whenever a squad name is entered, regardless of whether any friends are selected or exist
A squad can be submitted with zero friends selected (inviteUids: []) and the API accepts it
After creating an empty squad the user is redirected to the home page
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add beta Bicep parameter file and update @allowed constraint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a beta environment parameter file so that Bicep deployments can target a buddyburn-beta resource group using the same templates as dev and prod.
acceptanceCriteria: infra/main.bicep @allowed constraint is updated from ['dev', 'prod'] to ['dev', 'beta', 'prod']

infra/main.beta.bicepparam exists with: environment = 'beta', containerRegistryServer = 'burnbuddyacr.azurecr.io', imageTag = 'latest', keyVaultName = 'buddyburn-beta-kv', keyVaultResourceGroup = 'buddyburn-beta'
The file follows the same format and style as infra/main.prod.bicepparam
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create beta environment provisioning runbook script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a documented shell script that provisions the beta Azure environment (resource group, Key Vault, and initial Bicep deployment) so that any team member can set it up from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/provision-beta.sh exists and is executable
Script creates the buddyburn-beta resource group if it does not exist
Script creates the buddyburn-beta-kv Key Vault if it does not exist
Script includes commented instructions for manually mirroring required secrets from buddyburn-prod-kv to buddyburn-beta-kv (Firebase service account JSON, test user email, test user password)
Script runs az deployment group create using infra/main.beta.bicepparam
Script includes a --what-if dry-run step before the actual deployment with a confirmation prompt
Script has a header comment explaining usage and prerequisites (Azure CLI logged in, correct subscription selected)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write API integration test script parameterized by BASE_URL
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an automated integration test script that runs against a live API environment so that post-deployment health can be verified in CI.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/api-smoke.sh exists and is executable (or a TypeScript equivalent under tests/integration/)
Script accepts BASE_URL as an environment variable (e.g. BASE_URL=https://buddyburn-beta-api.example.com)

Script tests: GET /health returns 200, POST /users without Authorization header returns 401, GET /users/me without Authorization header returns 401, GET /friends without Authorization header returns 401

Script fetches a test Firebase ID token by retrieving test user email and password from Azure Key Vault (az keyvault secret show --vault-name <vault> --name <secret>) and signing in via the Firebase REST API (identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1/accounts:signInWithPassword)
Script tests: GET /users/me with valid token returns 200, GET /friends with valid token returns 200
Script exits with code 0 on all passing, non-zero on any failure with a clear error message
A README comment at the top of the script explains required env vars: BASE_URL, AZURE_KEYVAULT_NAME
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up Playwright and write E2E test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright browser tests for critical user flows so that the web app is verified end-to-end after each deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Playwright (@playwright/test) is added as a dev dependency at the repo root or under a new tests/e2e/ package
playwright.config.ts exists at the repo root (or tests/e2e/) and reads BASE_URL from the environment, sets retries: 1, and uploads traces on failure

Test file tests/e2e/smoke.spec.ts (or equivalent) exists with the following tests: (1) home/login page loads with status 200 and no console errors, (2) sign-up flow: fill email/password form and submit, expect redirect to home or confirmation, (3) login flow: sign in with test credentials fetched from AKV, expect redirect to home page, (4) navigate to /burn-squads/new, enter a squad name, submit without selecting any friends, expect redirect to home
Tests read BASE_URL from env; fall back to http://localhost:3000 for local dev
Test credentials (email, password) are read from environment variables INTEGRATION_TEST_EMAIL and INTEGRATION_TEST_PASSWORD which the workflow will populate from AKV
npx playwright test runs without TypeScript errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create deploy-api.yml: split API workflow with beta+prod deploy and integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow for the API that only triggers on API/shared changes, deploys to beta then production, and runs integration tests after each deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/deploy-api.yml exists
Workflow triggers on push to main with paths: services/api/**, packages/shared/**, infra/**
Workflow has a quality job: checkout, setup Node 24 with yarn cache, yarn install --frozen-lockfile, build shared package, typecheck, run API tests (yarn workspace api test)

Workflow has a build-and-push job (needs: quality): logs in to Azure using AZURE_CLIENT_ID/AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET/AZURE_TENANT_ID/AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID secrets, logs in to ACR, builds and pushes API Docker image tagged with short SHA to burnbuddyacr.azurecr.io/burnbuddy/api:<SHA>

Workflow has a deploy-beta job (needs: build-and-push): updates buddyburn-beta-api Container App to the new image, then runs tests/integration/api-smoke.sh with BASE_URL set to the beta API FQDN and AZURE_KEYVAULT_NAME=buddyburn-beta-kv

Workflow has a deploy-prod job (needs: deploy-beta): updates buddyburn-prod-api Container App to the new image, then runs tests/integration/api-smoke.sh with BASE_URL set to the prod API FQDN and AZURE_KEYVAULT_NAME=buddyburn-prod-kv
If integration tests fail for either environment the corresponding job fails and the pipeline stops
The original .github/workflows/deploy.yml is deleted in this same commit to avoid duplicate runs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create deploy-web.yml: split web workflow with beta+prod deploy and E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow for the web app that only triggers on web/shared changes, deploys to beta then production, and runs Playwright E2E tests after each deploy.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/deploy-web.yml exists
Workflow triggers on push to main with paths: apps/web/**, packages/shared/**
Workflow has a quality job: checkout, setup Node 24 with yarn cache, yarn install --frozen-lockfile, build shared package, typecheck
Workflow has a build-and-push job (needs: quality): logs in to Azure, logs in to ACR, builds and pushes web Docker image tagged with short SHA to burnbuddyacr.azurecr.io/burnbuddy/web:<SHA>

Workflow has a deploy-beta job (needs: build-and-push): updates buddyburn-beta-web Container App to the new image, fetches INTEGRATION_TEST_EMAIL and INTEGRATION_TEST_PASSWORD from buddyburn-beta-kv via az keyvault secret show, then runs npx playwright test with BASE_URL set to the beta web FQDN

Workflow has a deploy-prod job (needs: deploy-beta): updates buddyburn-prod-web Container App to the new image, fetches test credentials from buddyburn-prod-kv, then runs npx playwright test with BASE_URL set to the prod web FQDN
Playwright test artifacts (screenshots, traces) are uploaded using actions/upload-artifact on failure
If E2E tests fail for either environment the corresponding job fails and the pipeline stops
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reset progress.txt for beta-prod-deployment feature
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the progress log reset with a fresh header and codebase patterns section so that Ralph's future iterations have clean context for this new feature branch.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/progress.txt is replaced with a fresh file containing only a '## Codebase Patterns' header section populated with the most important patterns from the archived progress.txt (monorepo structure, auth pattern, API patterns, Firebase emulator setup)
The old story-by-story progress entries are removed (they are preserved in the archive)
Typecheck passes
